mnemonics: SIGnal SUSPEND: Sleep Until Signal Arrives
keyPoints: Atomically replaces the process signal mask and suspends execution
Used to wait for a specific signal while ensuring no race conditions
Typically used in conjunction with a signal handler that sets a global flag
commonMistakes: Not restoring the original signal mask after sigsuspend returns
Using it without a proper signal handler, causing indefinite suspension
Assuming sigsuspend preserves the original signal mask - it restores it upon return
